summary: Jeff Atwood announces an update to his WebFileManager CodeProject article, adding zip file support and column sorting capabilities. He includes both code-behind and inline code versions and notes a new dependency on SharpZipLib. The post briefly documents two technical limitations he discovered: SharpZipLib's inability to modify existing zip archives, and Firefox's lack of support for column alignment in the COLGROUP tag.
key_points: WebFileManager now supports zipping files and column sorting
SharpZipLib cannot modify existing zip archives, only create new ones
Firefox doesn't support alignment in COLGROUP tags, making column styling cumbersome
Both code-behind and inline code versions are now included in the solution
money_quotes: SharpZipLib, like the Java class it apes, is completely incapable of modifying an existing Zip archive.
CSS and HTML both kinda suck when referencing table columns. Cells and rows, yes, columns, not so much.
key_insight: A brief project update that documents practical limitations discovered in third-party libraries and browser implementations.
